title: Nivana
description: A Self Identifying Mental Health Assessment Tool that helps users evaluate their emotional well-being through guided assessments and personalized insights.

caseStudy.problem: Users need a private, structured way to assess mental wellness and track progress without friction.
architecture: React + Tailwind UI with modular feature pages
MongoDB data model for assessments and history
tradeoffs: MERN chosen for faster iteration and full-stack control
JWT-based auth for stateless, scalable sessions
Feature modules prioritized over a monolithic flow
results: 10+ modules built (auth, assessments, dashboards, mood tracking)
5+ wellness features including journaling and meditation

title: Shortest Path Finder
description: Visualizing Dijkstra's algorithm for finding the shortest path in a city using graph data structures.

caseStudy.problem: Help users understand shortest-path computation through an interactive, visual experience.
architecture: Client-side graph model with adjacency lists
UI layer for draggable nodes and live edges
tradeoffs: Chose Dijkstra for clarity and correctness
Focused on UX clarity over heavy animations
results: Faster traversal with optimized adjacency lists

title: Volunteer Management System
description: A robust platform for managing organization volunteers, events, and tracking participation.

caseStudy.problem: Enable teams to manage volunteers, schedules, and certificates in a single system.
architecture: PHP backend with MySQL data layer
tradeoffs: PHP chosen for quick server-side delivery
Lean jQuery UI to avoid heavy framework setup
Optimized pages over complex client state
